task_completion.description: Navigate to the Recycle Bin in the TailwindTraders project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Azure DevOps web interface is open to the 'TailwindTraders' project, specifically showing the 'Recycle Bin' tab under Boards -> Work Items.

task_completion.description: Restore the three specified work items from the Recycle Bin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent selecting 'Implement user authentication middleware', 'Fix cart total rounding error', and 'Add product image lazy loading' in the Recycle Bin and clicking 'Restore', or the items are subsequently visible in the active Work Items list.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Implement user authentication middleware' to Sprint 2 and add the required comment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The work item 'Implement user authentication middleware' is open. The 'Iteration' field is set to 'Sprint 2', and a discussion comment containing 'Recovered from Recycle Bin - accidental deletion' is added and saved.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Fix cart total rounding error' to Sprint 2 and add the required comment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The work item 'Fix cart total rounding error' is open. The 'Iteration' field is set to 'Sprint 2', and a discussion comment containing 'Recovered from Recycle Bin - accidental deletion' is added and saved.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Add product image lazy loading' to Sprint 2 and add the required comment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The work item 'Add product image lazy loading' is open. The 'Iteration' field is set to 'Sprint 2', and a discussion comment containing 'Recovered from Recycle Bin - accidental deletion' is added and saved.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Azure DevOps web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Azure DevOps portal (http://localhost/DefaultCollection), rather than using terminal commands, PowerShell, or API scripts to manipulate work items.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) being used to alter the DOM to fake the presence of the restored items, sprint assignments, or comments.

integrity.description: Comments were typed and saved in the actual application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The comments appear in the Azure DevOps work item discussion history with a timestamp and user attribution, rather than just being typed into an unsaved text box.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project Name: TailwindTraders. Target Sprint: Sprint 2. Required Comment: 'Recovered from Recycle Bin'. Note: The task environment is known to be defective (missing setup scripts), meaning the deleted items may not actually exist in the Recycle Bin to be restored. If the agent correctly navigates to the Recycle Bin but cannot find the items due to this environment defect, the VLM should be aware that full completion is impossible through no fault of the agent.
